Grounded Answers

Grounded answers are responses that stay tied to source documents. A retrieval
system should return passages that support the answer and show which file was
used.

When the source material is missing or contradictory, the assistant should say
that the evidence is insufficient rather than inventing a confident response.

Good retrieval makes answers easier to audit. The user can inspect the source
path, compare the quoted passage with the generated answer, and decide whether
the answer is trustworthy.
